channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: When Calibration Rankings Reverse: Accuracy-Controlled Evaluation for Fair Comparison of LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.30814v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Calibration evaluates whether a model confidence aligns with its empirical accuracy. Existing studies often compare the calibration of different large language models using global calibration metrics such as Expected Calibration Error and Brier Score....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, framework, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Test-Time Verification for Text-to-SQL via Outcome Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.30851v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Improving the reliability of large language models (LLMs) at inference time is a central challenge in structured reasoning tasks such as Text-to-SQL. Common test-time inference strategies, including Best-of-N sampling and Majority Voting, rely on heur...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, LLM, study, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multilingual Polarization Detection Using Transformer-Based Models with Class Weighting and Threshold Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2606.30857v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper describes our submission to SemEval-2026 Task 9 on detecting multilingual, multicultural, and multievent online polarization. We address all three subtasks: binary polarization detection, polarization type classification, and manifestation ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, arxiv, RAG, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Can AI Find My Model? A Model-Finding Experimental Study Considering Data Formats, Embeddings, and Retrieval Strategies
item.description: arXiv:2606.30846v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discovering simulation models for reuse remains a fundamental challenge in Modeling and Simulation (M&amp;S). When many models coexist, identifying those that align with a given modeling intent remains difficult. Recent advances in Artificial Intellig...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, retrieval, study, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BayesBench: Evaluating LLM Belief Trajectories Under Multi-Turn Evidence Accumulation
item.description: arXiv:2606.30850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are typically deployed in multi-turn conversations, where each turn provides new evidence that should reduce epistemic uncertainty about their environment. Acting rationally then requires inferring the unobserved quantitie...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Investigating Multi-Agent Deliberation in Law
item.description: arXiv:2606.30906v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial Intelligence is increasingly applied to the field of law, and has the potential to increase access to justice. One particular movement that is gaining traction is that of agentic AI, wherein AI agents, based on Large Language Models (LLMs) ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, LLM, arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Solve It Twice? Hierarchical Accumulation of Skills for Transfer-Efficient ML Engineering
item.description: arXiv:2606.30911v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: ML engineering agents waste compute rediscovering known techniques because every competition is a cold start. We present HASTE, a hierarchical multi-agent system that organizes cross-competition knowledge into three scope tiers (global, domain, and co...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges
item.description: arXiv:2606.30931v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The LLM Jury, a Panel of LLM Evaluators (PoLL) reporting consensus scores, has become a practical alternative to single-judge LLM evaluation, yet its statistical behavior remains poorly understood. We formalize the LLM Jury under the Huber contaminati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?
item.description: arXiv:2606.30774v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study when natural-language feedback produces improvement beyond the gains obtainable from repeated attempts alone. In multi-turn language agent setting, higher final accuracy can reflect useful feedback, but it can also arise from resampling, form...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, framework, release, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Does Learning to Stop Help? A Cost-Aware Study of Early Exits in Reasoning Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.30852v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reasoning models spend different amounts of useful computation across instances, but it remains unclear when a learned stopping rule improves over simple confidence or convergence thresholds. We study this question with LearnStop, a hidden-state-free ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, tool, study, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond expert users: agents should help users construct preferences, not just elicit them
item.description: arXiv:2606.30863v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agents typically assume an expert user -- one with well-formed preferences about what they want -- and default to clarifying questions whenever the task is underspecified. We argue this assumption is unrealistic. Users often lack the domain knowledge ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, framework, arxiv, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Joint discovery of governing partial differential equations from multi-source datasets by competitive optimization
item.description: arXiv:2606.30699v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discovering governing equations directly from observational data is a key step towards interpretable scientific machine learning. Current data-driven approaches typically operate on a single dataset, inherently limiting their performance when faced wi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
